articles.title: FOR THE LOVE OF CRAFT: VECTORIZE IMAGES IN FIGMA DESIGN AND DRAW
articles.summary: Figma Vectorize is an AI image editing tool that converts raster images into editable vectors with a single click, enabling designers to transform sketches, hand-drawn lettering, and textures directly within Figma Design and Draw. The tool streamlines workflows by eliminating the need to switch between applications or manually recreate artwork, while preserving the original character and enabling effortless refinement with Figma's existing design features. Vectorize is available to Full-seat users on Professional, Organization, and Enterprise plans with AI enabled.

articles.title: AN ARTIST BACKLASH JUST SAVED ADOBE ANIMATE
articles.summary: Adobe sparked backlash by announcing—with just a month's notice—that it would discontinue the 30-year-old Adobe Animate (formerly Flash), cutting off future downloads and access to users' work, despite the software's ongoing popularity and historic importance. After intense outcry from artists who warned of job losses and cultural damage, Adobe reversed the decision within 24 hours.

articles.title: TIM COOK REVEALED STANDOUT AI FEATURE FOR USERS, AND IT'S RUMORED TO GET EVEN BETTER
articles.summary: Tim Cook says visual intelligence is one of the most popular Apple Intelligence features, evolving in iOS 26 from a camera-only tool into a system-wide AI that works on screenshots to translate text, create links, and take actions across apps. He likely highlighted it because Apple plans to expand visual intelligence beyond the iPhone into future wearables like camera-equipped AirPods Pro and Apple Glasses, making it a core AI feature across devices.

articles.title: DOES EVERY DESIGNER ULTIMATELY HAVE TO MANAGE PEOPLE? THE IC PATH
articles.summary: The IC (Individual Contributor) path allows designers to advance their careers and increase their organizational impact without moving into people-management roles. Career progression typically follows Junior → Mid-level → Senior → Staff → Principal → Distinguished, with the path focusing primarily on craft and design problem-solving skills. As designers advance along the IC path, they tackle increasingly complex problems and gain greater independence.

articles.title: HOW TO USE CLAUDE CODE FOR UX WRITING
articles.summary: Claude Code is an AI tool that runs on your computer and can directly access and edit your files, unlike the web version, which only works with copy-pasted content. UX writers can use it to audit content, check consistency, and make text changes across multiple files using plain English commands without needing coding skills. The tool reduces dependence on developers for routine content updates and offers safety features, such as "Plan Mode," which prompts for permission before making changes.

articles.title: THE AI DELEGATION MATRIX: WHAT PARTS OF YOUR UI SHOULDN'T EXIST?
articles.summary: AI product design needs a clear, systematic way to decide when humans should lead, when AI should assist, and when tasks should be fully delegated, instead of vaguely adding AI everywhere. Using simple scores for risk and payoff, teams can map workflows to the right control mode to balance speed, safety, accountability, and real user value.

articles.title: AI WEB APP GENERATOR
articles.summary: Sketchflow.ai is an AI-powered web app generator that creates applications without coding.

articles.title: ACCESSIBILITY FOR EVERYONE (EBOOK)
articles.summary: This free eBook guides readers through the accessibility landscape: understand disability and impairment challenges, get a handle on key laws and guidelines, and learn how to plan, evaluate, and test.

articles.title: LOST FOR WORDS: WHY TEXT IN AI IMAGES STILL GOES WRONG
articles.summary: AI image generators struggle with text because they pattern-match visual shapes rather than understanding language, treating letters as pixels. While text generation accuracy has improved significantly in newer models like Ideogram and Recraft V3, editing existing text remains challenging because models must preserve surrounding context while modifying specific regions. Designers currently work around these limitations by using text-focused generators, adding typography in traditional design tools after image creation, or employing hybrid workflows across multiple platforms.

articles.title: HERE'S HOW APPLE MAY PROTECT THE DISPLAY OF THE IPHONE FOLD
articles.summary: Apple is reportedly taking extra steps to avoid past foldable-phone failures by exploring a tougher built-in screen protector for the upcoming iPhone Fold, alongside design changes to reduce internal cable damage. Unlike Samsung's PET film, Apple may use a more scratch-resistant (but pricier) transparent polyimide layer to better protect the ultra-thin folding glass.

articles.title: TURNING JOBS INTO JOY – BRANDON CONSULTANTS REBRANDS FABULOSA FOR THE TIKTOK ERA
articles.summary: Fabulosa has been rebranded from a functional cleaning product into a bold lifestyle brand that celebrates the joy of cleaning, tapping into the booming “cleanfluencer” culture on social media. The new identity uses playful, unapologetically vibrant design and a flexible system to bring consistency and personality across a large product range while strengthening recognition and emotional appeal.

articles.title: ARTIST CREATES NOSTALGIC PIXEL TRIBUTES THAT CAPTURE GAMING'S GOLDEN AGE DESIGN
articles.summary: Jesús Campos "Nerkin" is a senior pixel artist at The Game Kitchen who specializes in nostalgic pixel art.

articles.title: 'BRANDS SHOULD MIRROR HOW PEOPLE FEEL': MEET THE STUDIO TRANSFORMING ADVERTISING
articles.summary: Bindery, a New York-based agency led by Emmy-winning filmmaker Greg Beauchamp, works with brands like CarGurus and Colgate to create campaigns that are strategic, emotional, and culturally relevant.
